{
  "weights": {
    "R1.1": 0.7,

    "R1.3": 0.7,
    "R1.4": 0.3,
    "R1.5": 0.3,
    "R1.6": 0.7,
    "R1.7": 0.3,
    "RX.1": 1.0,
    "RX.2": 1.0,
    "RX.3": 1.0,
    "RX.4": 1.0,
    "RX.5": 1.0
  },
  "active_set": ["R1.1", "R1.2", "R1.3", "R1.6", "RX.1", "RX.2", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "The section asserts a 'novel approach' and a specific gap (concurrent writes in geo-distributed settings); a review must assess whether the thesis is coherent, the gap real, and the contribution claim defensible given what is actually shown.",
    "R1.2": "Several internal logical claims demand scrutiny: 'eventual consistency in the absence of partitions' is unusual phrasing (EC is normally characterized relative to failures, not their absence); the bounded-staleness formula max(2T, 50ms) and its derivation are referenced but not defended here; and the claim of 'no client-visible inconsistencies' after partition healing needs to follow logically from the protocol guarantees.",
    "R1.3": "The section contains concrete quantitative claims (p99 8.4ms / 142ms at 50k writes/sec; 3-region partition survival for 47 min with no inconsistencies) that must be checked for alignment with the experimental setup described and with the formal guarantee in Section 4.",
    "R1.6": "A section review centrally includes prose quality; the opening repeats 'challenge' twice in adjacent sentences, the transition from protocol description to motivation paragraph is inverted from reader-expectation order, and tightening would improve clarity."
  },
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "Should this review focus primarily on technical correctness (logical consistency of claims, evidence-claim alignment, validity of the bounded-staleness guarantee) rather than writing quality (prose clarity, flow, style)?"
}

rationale.R1.1: The section asserts a 'novel approach' and a specific gap (concurrent writes in geo-distributed settings); a review must assess whether the thesis is coherent, the gap real, and the contribution claim defensible given what is actually shown.
rationale.R1.2: Several internal logical claims demand scrutiny: 'eventual consistency in the absence of partitions' is unusual phrasing (EC is normally characterized relative to failures, not their absence); the bounded-staleness formula max(2T, 50ms) and its derivation are referenced but not defended here; and the claim of 'no client-visible inconsistencies' after partition healing needs to follow logically from the protocol guarantees.
rationale.R1.3: The section contains concrete quantitative claims (p99 8.4ms / 142ms at 50k writes/sec; 3-region partition survival for 47 min with no inconsistencies) that must be checked for alignment with the experimental setup described and with the formal guarantee in Section 4.
rationale.R1.6: A section review centrally includes prose quality; the opening repeats 'challenge' twice in adjacent sentences, the transition from protocol description to motivation paragraph is inverted from reader-expectation order, and tightening would improve clarity.

parsed.clarification_question: Should this review focus primarily on technical correctness (logical consistency of claims, evidence-claim alignment, validity of the bounded-staleness guarantee) rather than writing quality (prose clarity, flow, style)?
